NKJV.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
NKJV.2: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ who are in Colosse: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.3: We give thanks to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,
NKJV.4: since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of your love for all the saints;
NKJV.5: because of the hope which is laid up for you in heaven, of which you heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel,
NKJV.6: which has come to you, as it has also in all the world, and is bringing forth fruit, as it is also among you since the day you heard and knew the grace of God in truth;
NKJV.7: as you also learned from Epaphras, our dear fellow servant, who is a faithful minister of Christ on your behalf,
NKJV.8: who also declared to us your love in the Spirit.
NKJV.9: For this reason we also, since the day we heard it, do not cease to pray for you, and to ask that you may be filled with the knowledge of His will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding;
NKJV.10: that you may walk worthy of the Lord, fully pleasing Him, being fruitful in every good work and increasing in the knowledge of God;
NKJV.11: strengthened with all might, according to His glorious power, for all patience and longsuffering with joy;
NKJV.12: giving thanks to the Father who has qualified us to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in the light.
NKJV.13: He has delivered us from the power of darkness and conveyed us into the kingdom of the Son of His love,
NKJV.14: in whom we have redemption through His blood, the forgiveness of sins.
NKJV.15: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn over all creation.
NKJV.16: For by Him all things were created that are in heaven and that are on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers. All things were created through Him and for Him.
NKJV.17: And He is before all things, and in Him all things consist.
NKJV.18: And He is the head of the body, the church, who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in all things He may have the preeminence.
NKJV.19: For it pleased the Father that in Him all the fullness should dwell,
NKJV.20: and by Him to reconcile all things to Himself, by Him, whether things on earth or things in heaven, having made peace through the blood of His cross.
NKJV.21: And you, who once were alienated and enemies in your mind by wicked works, yet now He has reconciled
NKJV.22: in the body of His flesh through death, to present you holy, and blameless, and above reproach in His sight—
NKJV.23: if indeed you continue in the faith, grounded and steadfast, and are not moved away from the hope of the gospel which you heard, which was preached to every creature under heaven, of which I, Paul, became a minister.
NKJV.24: I now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up in my flesh what is lacking in the afflictions of Christ, for the sake of His body, which is the church,
NKJV.25: of which I became a minister according to the stewardship from God which was given to me for you, to fulfill the word of God,
NKJV.26: the mystery which has been hidden from ages and from generations, but now has been revealed to His saints.
NKJV.27: To them God willed to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles: which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
NKJV.28: Him we preach, warning every man and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.29: To this end I also labor, striving according to His working which works in me mightily.

ESV.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
ESV.2: To the saints and faithful brothers  in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
ESV.3: We always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,
ESV.4: since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love that you have for all the saints,
ESV.5: because of the hope laid up for you in heaven. Of this you have heard before in the word of the truth, the gospel,
ESV.6: which has come to you, as indeed in the whole world it is bearing fruit and increasing — as it also does among you, since the day you heard it and understood the grace of God in truth,
ESV.7: just as you learned it from Epaphras our beloved fellow servant.  He is a faithful minister of Christ on your  behalf
ESV.8: and has made known to us your love in the Spirit.
ESV.9: And so, from the day we heard, we have not ceased to pray for you, asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
ESV.10: so as to walk in a manner worthy of the Lord, fully pleasing to him: bearing fruit in every good work and increasing in the knowledge of God;
ESV.11: being strengthened with all power, according to his glorious might, for all endurance and patience with joy;
ESV.12: giving thanks  to the Father, who has qualified you  to share in the inheritance of the saints in light.
ESV.13: He has delivered us from the domain of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of his beloved Son,
ESV.14: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
ESV.15: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.
ESV.16: For by  him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities — all things were created through him and for him.
ESV.17: And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
ESV.18: And he is the head of the body, the church. He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in everything he might be preeminent.
ESV.19: For in him all the fullness of God was pleased to dwell,
ESV.20: and through him to reconcile to himself all things, whether on earth or in heaven, making peace by the blood of his cross.
ESV.21: And you, who once were alienated and hostile in mind, doing evil deeds,
ESV.22: he has now reconciled in his body of flesh by his death, in order to present you holy and blameless and above reproach before him,
ESV.23: if indeed you continue in the faith, stable and steadfast, not shifting from the hope of the gospel that you heard, which has been proclaimed in all creation  under heaven, and of which I, Paul, became a minister.
ESV.24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and in my flesh I am filling up what is lacking in Christ's afflictions for the sake of his body, that is, the church,
ESV.25: of which I became a minister according to the stewardship from God that was given to me for you, to make the word of God fully known,
ESV.26: the mystery hidden for ages and generations but now revealed to his saints.
ESV.27: To them God chose to make known how great among the Gentiles are the riches of the glory of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
ESV.28: Him we proclaim, warning everyone and teaching everyone with all wisdom, that we may present everyone mature in Christ.
ESV.29: For this I toil, struggling with all his energy that he powerfully works within me.

NASB.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
NASB.2: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ [who are] at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
NASB.3: We give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,
NASB.4: since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and the love which you have for all the saints;
NASB.5: because of the hope laid up for you in heaven, of which you previously heard in the word of truth, the gospel
NASB.6: which has come to you, just as in all the world also it is constantly bearing fruit and increasing, even as [it has been doing] in you also since the day you heard [of it] and understood the grace of God in truth;
NASB.7: just as you learned [it] from Epaphras, our beloved fellow bond-servant, who is a faithful servant of Christ on our behalf,
NASB.8: and he also informed us of your love in the Spirit.
NASB.9: For this reason also, since the day we heard [of it], we have not ceased to pray for you and to ask that you may be filled with the knowledge of His will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
NASB.10: so that you will walk in a manner worthy of the Lord, to please [Him] in all respects, bearing fruit in every good work and increasing in the knowledge of God;
NASB.11: strengthened with all power, according to His glorious might, for the attaining of all steadfastness and patience; joyously
NASB.12: giving thanks to the Father, who has qualified us to share in the inheritance of the saints in Light.
NASB.13: For He rescued us from the domain of darkness, and transferred us to the kingdom of His beloved Son,
NASB.14: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
NASB.15: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.
NASB.16: For by Him all things were created, [both] in the heavens and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities-- all things have been created through Him and for Him.
NASB.17: He is before all things, and in Him all things hold together.
NASB.18: He is also head of the body, the church; and He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that He Himself will come to have first place in everything.
NASB.19: For it was the [Father's] good pleasure for all the fullness to dwell in Him,
NASB.20: and through Him to reconcile all things to Himself, having made peace through the blood of His cross; through Him, [I say], whether things on earth or things in heaven.
NASB.21: And although you were formerly alienated and hostile in mind, [engaged] in evil deeds,
NASB.22: yet He has now reconciled you in His fleshly body through death, in order to present you before Him holy and blameless and beyond reproach--
NASB.23: if indeed you continue in the faith firmly established and steadfast, and not moved away from the hope of the gospel that you have heard, which was proclaimed in all creation under heaven, and of which I, Paul, was made a minister.
NASB.24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and in my flesh I do my share on behalf of His body, which is the church, in filling up what is lacking in Christ's afflictions.
NASB.25: Of [this church] I was made a minister according to the stewardship from God bestowed on me for your benefit, so that I might fully carry out the [preaching of] the word of God,
NASB.26: [that is], the mystery which has been hidden from the [past] ages and generations, but has now been manifested to His saints,
NASB.27: to whom God willed to make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
NASB.28: We proclaim Him, admonishing every man and teaching every man with all wisdom, so that we may present every man complete in Christ.
NASB.29: For this purpose also I labor, striving according to His power, which mightily works within me.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, chosen by the will of God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from our brother Timothy.
NLT.2: We are writing to God’s holy people in the city of Colosse, who are faithful brothers and sisters in Christ. May God our Father give you grace and peace.
NLT.3: We always pray for you, and we give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NLT.4: For we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and your love for all of God’s people,
NLT.5: which come from your confident hope of what God has reserved for you in heaven. You have had this expectation ever since you first heard the truth of the Good News.
NLT.6: This same Good News that came to you is going out all over the world. It is bearing fruit everywhere by changing lives, just as it changed your lives from the day you first heard and understood the truth about God’s wonderful grace.
NLT.7: You learned about the Good News from Epaphras, our beloved co-worker. He is Christ’s faithful servant, and he is helping us on your behalf.
NLT.8: He has told us about the love for others that the Holy Spirit has given you.
NLT.9: So we have not stopped praying for you since we first heard about you. We ask God to give you complete knowledge of his will and to give you spiritual wisdom and understanding.
NLT.10: Then the way you live will always honor and please the Lord, and your lives will produce every kind of good fruit. All the while, you will grow as you learn to know God better and better.
NLT.11: We also pray that you will be strengthened with all his glorious power so you will have all the endurance and patience you need. May you be filled with joy,
NLT.12: always thanking the Father. He has enabled you to share in the inheritance that belongs to his people, who live in the light.
NLT.13: For he has rescued us from the kingdom of darkness and transferred us into the Kingdom of his dear Son,
NLT.14: who purchased our freedom and forgave our sins.
NLT.15:
Christ is the visible image of the invisible God.
He existed before anything was created and is supreme over all creation,
NLT.16:
for through him God created everything
in the heavenly realms and on earth.
He made the things we can see
and the things we can’t see —
such as thrones, kingdoms, rulers, and authorities in the unseen world.
Everything was created through him and for him.
NLT.17:
He existed before anything else,
and he holds all creation together.
NLT.18:
Christ is also the head of the church,
which is his body.
He is the beginning,
supreme over all who rise from the dead.
So he is first in everything.
NLT.19:
For God in all his fullness
was pleased to live in Christ,
NLT.20:
and through him God reconciled
everything to himself.
He made peace with everything in heaven and on earth
by means of Christ’s blood on the cross.
NLT.21: This includes you who were once far away from God. You were his enemies, separated from him by your evil thoughts and actions.
NLT.22: Yet now he has reconciled you to himself through the death of Christ in his physical body. As a result, he has brought you into his own presence, and you are holy and blameless as you stand before him without a single fault.
NLT.23: But you must continue to believe this truth and stand firmly in it. Don’t drift away from the assurance you received when you heard the Good News. The Good News has been preached all over the world, and I, Paul, have been appointed as God’s servant to proclaim it.
NLT.24: I am glad when I suffer for you in my body, for I am participating in the sufferings of Christ that continue for his body, the church.
NLT.25: God has given me the responsibility of serving his church by proclaiming his entire message to you.
NLT.26: This message was kept secret for centuries and generations past, but now it has been revealed to God’s people.
NLT.27: For God wanted them to know that the riches and glory of Christ are for you Gentiles, too. And this is the secret: Christ lives in you. This gives you assurance of sharing his glory.
NLT.28: So we tell others about Christ, warning everyone and teaching everyone with all the wisdom God has given us. We want to present them to God, perfect in their relationship to Christ.
NLT.29: That’s why I work and struggle so hard, depending on Christ’s mighty power that works within me.

WEB.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
WEB.2: to the saints and faithful brothers  in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.3: We give thanks to God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,
WEB.4: having heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which you have toward all the saints,
WEB.5: because of the hope which is laid up for you in the heavens, of which you heard before in the word of the truth of the Good News,
WEB.6: which has come to you; even as it is in all the world and is bearing fruit and growing, as it does in you also, since the day you heard and knew the grace of God in truth;
WEB.7: even as you learned of Epaphras our beloved fellow servant, who is a faithful servant of Christ on our behalf,
WEB.8: who also declared to us your love in the Spirit.
WEB.9: For this cause, we also, since the day we heard this, don’t cease praying and making requests for you, that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
WEB.10: that you may walk worthily of the Lord, to please him in all respects, bearing fruit in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;
WEB.11: strengthened with all power, according to the might of his glory, for all endurance and perseverance with joy;
WEB.12: giving thanks to the Father, who made us fit to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light;
WEB.13: who delivered us out of the power of darkness, and translated us into the Kingdom of the Son of his love;
WEB.14: in whom we have our redemption,  the forgiveness of our sins;
WEB.15: who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.
WEB.16: For by him all things were created, in the heavens and on the earth, things visible and things invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers; all things have been created through him, and for him.
WEB.17: He is before all things, and in him all things are held together.
WEB.18: He is the head of the body, the assembly, who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.
WEB.19: For all the fullness was pleased to dwell in him;
WEB.20: and through him to reconcile all things to himself, by him, whether things on the earth, or things in the heavens, having made peace through the blood of his cross.
WEB.21: You, being in past times alienated and enemies in your mind in your evil deeds,
WEB.22: yet now he has reconciled in the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and without defect and blameless before him,
WEB.23: if it is so that you continue in the faith, grounded and steadfast, and not moved away from the hope of the Good News which you heard, which is being proclaimed in all creation under heaven; of which I, Paul, was made a servant.
WEB.24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and fill up on my part that which is lacking of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the assembly;
WEB.25: of which I was made a servant, according to the stewardship of God which was given me toward you, to fulfill the word of God,
WEB.26: the mystery which has been hidden for ages and generations. But now it has been revealed to his saints,
WEB.27: to whom God was pleased to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory;
WEB.28: whom we proclaim, admonishing every man and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus;
WEB.29: for which I also labor, striving according to his working, which works in me mightily.

CSB17.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by God’s will, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and Timothy <sup>ⓛ</sup> our brother:
CSB17.2: To the saints in Christ at Colossae, who are faithful brothers and sisters. Grace to you and peace from God our Father. <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.3: We always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,
CSB17.4: for we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love you have for all the saints
CSB17.5: because of the hope reserved for you in heaven. You have already heard about this hope in the word of truth, <sup>ⓜ</sup> the gospel
CSB17.6: that has come to you. It is bearing fruit <sup>ⓝ</sup> and growing all over the world, <sup>ⓐ</sup> just as it has among you since the day you heard it and came to truly appreciate <sup>ⓑ</sup> God’s grace. <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.7: You learned this from Epaphras, <sup>ⓓ</sup> our dearly loved fellow servant. He is a faithful minister of Christ <sup>ⓔ</sup> on your <sup>[3]</sup> behalf,
CSB17.8: and he has told us about your love in the Spirit. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.9: For this reason also, since the day we heard this, we haven’t stopped praying for you. We are asking <sup>ⓖ</sup> that you may be filled with the knowledge <sup>ⓗ</sup> of his will <sup>ⓘ</sup> in all wisdom and spiritual understanding, <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.10: so that you may walk worthy of the Lord, fully pleasing to him: bearing fruit in every good work <sup>ⓙ</sup> and growing in the knowledge of God, <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.11: being strengthened <sup>ⓛ</sup> with all power, <sup>ⓜ</sup> according to his glorious <sup>ⓝ</sup> might, so that you may have great endurance and patience, joyfully
CSB17.12: giving thanks to the Father, who has enabled you <sup>[5]</sup> to share in the saints’ inheritance in the light. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.13: He has rescued <sup>ⓑ</sup> us from the domain of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom <sup>ⓒ</sup> of the Son <sup>ⓓ</sup> he loves. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.14: In him we have redemption, <sup>[6]</sup> the forgiveness of sins.
CSB17.15:
He is the image of the invisible God, ⓕ
the firstborn over all creation. ⓖ
CSB17.16:
For everything was created by him, ⓗ
in heaven and on earth,
the visible and the invisible,
whether thrones or dominions
or rulers or authorities —
all things have been created through him and for him. ⓘ
CSB17.17:
He is before all things, ⓙ
and by him all things ⓚ hold together.
CSB17.18:
He is also the head of the body, the church;
he is the beginning, ⓛ
the firstborn from the dead, ⓜ
so that he might come to have
first place in everything.
CSB17.19:
For God was pleased to have
all his fullness ⓝ dwell in him, ⓐ
CSB17.20:
and through him to reconcile
everything to himself,
whether things on earth or things in heaven,
by making peace ⓑ
through his blood, ⓒ shed on the cross. [7] ⓓ
CSB17.21: Once you were alienated and hostile in your minds expressed in your evil actions.
CSB17.22: But now he has reconciled you by his physical body through his death, <sup>ⓔ</sup> to present you holy, faultless, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and blameless before him <sup>ⓖ</sup>  —
CSB17.23: if indeed you remain grounded and steadfast in the faith <sup>ⓗ</sup> and are not shifted away from the hope <sup>ⓘ</sup> of the gospel that you heard. This gospel has been proclaimed in all creation <sup>ⓙ</sup> under heaven, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and I, Paul, <sup>ⓛ</sup> have become a servant of it.
CSB17.24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings <sup>ⓜ</sup> for you, and I am completing in my flesh what is lacking in Christ’s afflictions for his body, <sup>ⓝ</sup> that is, the church.
CSB17.25: I have become its servant, according to God’s commission that was given to me for you, to make the word of God fully known,
CSB17.26: the mystery hidden for ages and generations but now revealed to his saints.
CSB17.27: God wanted to make known among the Gentiles the glorious wealth of this mystery, which is Christ <sup>ⓐ</sup> in you, the hope <sup>ⓑ</sup> of glory.
CSB17.28: We proclaim him, warning and teaching everyone with all wisdom, so that we may present everyone mature in Christ.
CSB17.29: I labor for this, striving <sup>ⓒ</sup> with his strength that works powerfully in me. <sup>ⓓ</sup>

AMP.1: Paul, an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Christ Jesus (the Messiah, the Anointed) by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
AMP.2: To the saints and faithful believers in Christ [who are] at Colossae: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father.
AMP.3: We give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, as we pray always for you,
AMP.4: for we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus [how you lean on Him with absolute confidence in His power, wisdom, and goodness], and of the [unselfish] love which you have for all the saints (God's people);
AMP.5: because of the [confident] hope [of experiencing that] which is reserved and waiting for you in heaven. You previously heard of this hope in the message of truth, the gospel [regarding salvation]
AMP.6: which has come to you. Indeed, just as in the whole world the gospel is constantly bearing fruit and spreading [by God's power], just as it has been doing among you ever since the day you first heard of it and understood the grace of God in truth [becoming thoroughly and deeply acquainted with it].
AMP.7: You learned it from [our representative] Epaphras, our beloved fellow bond-servant, who is a faithful minister of Christ on our behalf
AMP.8: and he also has told us of your love [well-grounded and nurtured] in the [Holy] Spirit.
AMP.9: For this reason, since the day we heard about it, we have not stopped praying for you, asking [specifically] that you may be filled with the knowledge of His will in all spiritual wisdom [with insight into His purposes], and in understanding [of spiritual things],
AMP.10: so that you will walk in a manner worthy of the Lord [displaying admirable character, moral courage, and personal integrity], to [fully] please Him in all things, bearing fruit in every good work and steadily growing in the knowledge of God [with deeper faith, clearer insight and fervent love for His precepts];
AMP.11: [we pray that you may be] strengthened and invigorated with all power, according to His glorious might, to attain every kind of endurance and patience with joy;
AMP.12: giving thanks to the Father, who has qualified us to share in the inheritance of the saints (God's people) in the Light.
AMP.13: For He has rescued us and has drawn us to Himself from the dominion of darkness, and has transferred us to the kingdom of His beloved Son,
AMP.14: in whom we have redemption [because of His sacrifice, resulting in] the forgiveness of our sins [and the cancellation of sins' penalty].
AMP.15: He is the exact living image [the essential manifestation] of the unseen God [the visible representation of the invisible], the firstborn [the preeminent one, the sovereign, and the originator] of all creation.
AMP.16: For by Him all things were created in heaven and on earth, [things] visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities; all things were created and exist through Him [that is, by His activity] and for Him.
AMP.17: And He Himself existed and is before all things, and in Him all things hold together. [His is the controlling, cohesive force of the universe.]
AMP.18: He is also the head [the life-source and leader] of the body, the church; and He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that He Himself will occupy the first place [He will stand supreme and be preeminent] in everything.
AMP.19: For it pleased the Father for all the fullness [of deity—the sum total of His essence, all His perfection, powers, and attributes] to dwell [permanently] in Him (the Son),
AMP.20: and through [the intervention of] the Son to reconcile all things to Himself, making peace [with believers] through the blood of His cross; through Him, [I say,] whether things on earth or things in heaven.
AMP.21: And although you were at one time estranged and alienated and hostile-minded [toward Him], participating in evil things,
AMP.22: yet Christ has now reconciled you [to God] in His physical body through death, in order to present you before the Father holy and blameless and beyond reproach—
AMP.23: [and He will do this] if you continue in the faith, well-grounded and steadfast, and not shifting away from the [confident] hope [that is a result] of the gospel that you have heard, which was proclaimed in all creation under heaven, and of which [gospel] I, Paul, was made a minister.
AMP.24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings on your behalf. And with my own body I supplement whatever is lacking [on our part] of Christ's afflictions, on behalf of His body, which is the church.
AMP.25: In this church I was made a minister according to the stewardship which God entrusted to me for your sake, so that I might make the word of God fully known [among you]—
AMP.26: that is, the mystery which was hidden [from angels and mankind] for ages and generations, but has now been revealed to His saints (God's people).
AMP.27: God [in His eternal plan] chose to make known to them how great for the Gentiles are the riches of the glory of this mystery, which is Christ in and among you, the hope and guarantee of [realizing the] glory.
AMP.28: We proclaim Him, warning and instructing everyone in all wisdom [that is, with comprehensive insight into the word and purposes of God], so that we may present every person complete in Christ [mature, fully trained, and perfect in Him—the Anointed].
AMP.29: For this I labor [often to the point of exhaustion], striving with His power and energy, which so greatly works within me.

MSG.1: I, Paul, have been sent on special assignment by Christ as part of God's master plan. Together with my friend Timothy,
MSG.2: I greet the Christians and stalwart followers of Christ who live in Colosse. May everything good from God our Father be yours!
MSG.3: Our prayers for you are always spilling over into thanksgivings. We can't quit thanking God our Father and Jesus our Messiah for you!
MSG.4: We keep getting reports on your steady faith in Christ, our Jesus, and the love you continuously extend to all Christians.
MSG.5: The lines of purpose in your lives never grow slack, tightly tied as they are to your future in heaven, kept taut by hope. The Message is as true among you today as when you first heard it. It doesn't diminish or weaken over time.
MSG.6: It's the same all over the world. The Message bears fruit and gets larger and stronger, just as it has in you. From the very first day you heard and recognized the truth of what God is doing, you've been hungry for more.
MSG.7: It's as vigorous in you now as when you learned it from our friend and close associate Epaphras. He is one reliable worker for Christ! I could always depend on him.
MSG.8: He's the one who told us how thoroughly love had been worked into your lives by the Spirit.
MSG.9: Be assured that from the first day we heard of you, we haven't stopped praying for you, asking God to give you wise minds and spirits attuned to his will, and so acquire a thorough understanding of the ways in which God works.
MSG.10: We pray that you'll live well for the Master, making him proud of you as you work hard in his orchard. As you learn more and more how God works, you will learn how to do your work.
MSG.11: We pray that you'll have the strength to stick it out over the long haul—not the grim strength of gritting your teeth but the glory-strength God gives. It is strength that endures the unendurable and spills over into joy,
MSG.12: thanking the Father who makes us strong enough to take part in everything bright and beautiful that he has for us.
MSG.13: God rescued us from dead-end alleys and dark dungeons. He's set us up in the kingdom of the Son he loves so much,
MSG.14: the Son who got us out of the pit we were in, got rid of the sins we were doomed to keep repeating.
MSG.15: We look at this Son and see the God who cannot be seen. We look at this Son and see God's original purpose in everything created.
MSG.16: For everything, absolutely everything, above and below, visible and invisible, rank after rank after rank of angels—everything got started in him and finds its purpose in him.
MSG.17: He was there before any of it came into existence and holds it all together right up to this moment.
MSG.18: And when it comes to the church, he organizes and holds it together, like a head does a body. He was supreme in the beginning and—leading the resurrection parade—he is supreme in the end. From beginning to end he's there, towering far above everything, everyone.
MSG.19: So spacious is he, so roomy, that everything of God finds its proper place in him without crowding.
MSG.20: Not only that, but all the broken and dislocated pieces of the universe—people and things, animals and atoms—get properly fixed and fit together in vibrant harmonies, all because of his death, his blood that poured down from the Cross.
MSG.21: You yourselves are a case study of what he does. At one time you all had your backs turned to God, thinking rebellious thoughts of him, giving him trouble every chance you got.
MSG.22: But now, by giving himself completely at the Cross, actually dying for you, Christ brought you over to God's side and put your lives together, whole and holy in his presence.
MSG.23: You don't walk away from a gift like that! You stay grounded and steady in that bond of trust, constantly tuned in to the Message, careful not to be distracted or diverted. There is no other Message—just this one. Every creature under heaven gets this same Message. I, Paul, am a messenger of this Message.
MSG.24: I want you to know how glad I am that it's me sitting here in this jail and not you. There's a lot of suffering to be entered into in this world—the kind of suffering Christ takes on. I welcome the chance to take my share in the church's part of that suffering.
MSG.25: When I became a servant in this church, I experienced this suffering as a sheer gift, God's way of helping me serve you, laying out the whole truth.
MSG.26: This mystery has been kept in the dark for a long time, but now it's out in the open.
MSG.27: God wanted everyone, not just Jews, to know this rich and glorious secret inside and out, regardless of their background, regardless of their religious standing. The mystery in a nutshell is just this: Christ is in you, therefore you can look forward to sharing in God's glory. It's that simple. That is the substance of our Message.
MSG.28: We preach Christ, warning people not to add to the Message. We teach in a spirit of profound common sense so that we can bring each person to maturity. To be mature is to be basic. Christ! No more, no less.
MSG.29: That's what I'm working so hard at day after day, year after year, doing my best with the energy God so generously gives me.

NIV.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
NIV.2:
To the holy and faithful brothers in Christ at Colosse:
Grace and peace to you from God our Father.
NIV.3:
Thanksgiving and Prayer
We always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,
NIV.4: because we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love you have for all the saints—
NIV.5: the faith and love that spring from the hope that is stored up for you in heaven and that you have already heard about in the word of truth, the gospel
NIV.6: that has come to you. All over the world this gospel is bearing fruit and growing, just as it has been doing among you since the day you heard it and understood God’s grace in all its truth.
NIV.7: You learned it from Epaphras, our dear fellow servant, who is a faithful minister of Christ on our behalf,
NIV.8: and who also told us of your love in the Spirit.
NIV.9: For this reason, since the day we heard about you, we have not stopped praying for you and asking God to fill you with the knowledge of his will through all spiritual wisdom and understanding.
NIV.10: And we pray this in order that you may live a life worthy of the Lord and may please him in every way: bearing fruit in every good work, growing in the knowledge of God,
NIV.11: being strengthened with all power according to his glorious might so that you may have great endurance and patience, and joyfully
NIV.12: giving thanks to the Father, who has qualified you to share in the inheritance of the saints in the kingdom of light.
NIV.13: For he has rescued us from the dominion of darkness and brought us into the kingdom of the Son he loves,
NIV.14: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
NIV.15:
The Supremacy of Christ
He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn over all creation.
NIV.16: For by him all things were created: things in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or powers or rulers or authorities; all things were created by him and for him.
NIV.17: He is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
NIV.18: And he is the head of the body, the church; he is the beginning and the firstborn from among the dead, so that in everything he might have the supremacy.
NIV.19: For God was pleased to have all his fullness dwell in him,
NIV.20: and through him to reconcile to himself all things, whether things on earth or things in heaven, by making peace through his blood, shed on the cross.
NIV.21: Once you were alienated from God and were enemies in your minds because of your evil behavior.
NIV.22: But now he has reconciled you by Christ’s physical body through death to present you holy in his sight, without blemish and free from accusation—
NIV.23: if you continue in your faith, established and firm, not moved from the hope held out in the gospel. This is the gospel that you heard and that has been proclaimed to every creature under heaven, and of which I, Paul, have become a servant.
NIV.24:
Paul’s Labor for the Church
Now I rejoice in what was suffered for you, and I fill up in my flesh what is still lacking in regard to Christ’s afflictions, for the sake of his body, which is the church.
NIV.25: I have become its servant by the commission God gave me to present to you the word of God in its fullness—
NIV.26: the mystery that has been kept hidden for ages and generations, but is now disclosed to the saints.
NIV.27: To them God has chosen to make known among the Gentiles the glorious riches of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
NIV.28: We proclaim him, admonishing and teaching everyone with all wisdom, so that we may present everyone perfect in Christ.
NIV.29: To this end I labor, struggling with all his energy, which so powerfully works in me.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
NRSVCE.2: To the saints and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ in Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
NRSVCE.3: In our prayers for you we always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ,
NRSVCE.4: for we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love that you have for all the saints,
NRSVCE.5: because of the hope laid up for you in heaven. You have heard of this hope before in the word of the truth, the gospel
NRSVCE.6: that has come to you. Just as it is bearing fruit and growing in the whole world, so it has been bearing fruit among yourselves from the day you heard it and truly comprehended the grace of God.
NRSVCE.7: This you learned from Epaphras, our beloved fellow servant. He is a faithful minister of Christ on your behalf,
NRSVCE.8: and he has made known to us your love in the Spirit.
NRSVCE.9: For this reason, since the day we heard it, we have not ceased praying for you and asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of God’s will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
NRSVCE.10: so that you may lead lives worthy of the Lord, fully pleasing to him, as you bear fruit in every good work and as you grow in the knowledge of God.
NRSVCE.11: May you be made strong with all the strength that comes from his glorious power, and may you be prepared to endure everything with patience, while joyfully
NRSVCE.12: giving thanks to the Father, who has enabled you to share in the inheritance of the saints in the light.
NRSVCE.13: He has rescued us from the power of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of his beloved Son,
NRSVCE.14: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
NRSVCE.15: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation;
NRSVCE.16: for in him all things in heaven and on earth were created, things visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or powers — all things have been created through him and for him.
NRSVCE.17: He himself is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
NRSVCE.18: He is the head of the body, the church; he is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that he might come to have first place in everything.
NRSVCE.19: For in him all the fullness of God was pleased to dwell,
NRSVCE.20: and through him God was pleased to reconcile to himself all things, whether on earth or in heaven, by making peace through the blood of his cross.
NRSVCE.21: And you who were once estranged and hostile in mind, doing evil deeds,
NRSVCE.22: he has now reconciled in his fleshly body through death, so as to present you holy and blameless and irreproachable before him —
NRSVCE.23: provided that you continue securely established and steadfast in the faith, without shifting from the hope promised by the gospel that you heard, which has been proclaimed to every creature under heaven. I, Paul, became a servant of this gospel.
NRSVCE.24: I am now rejoicing in my sufferings for your sake, and in my flesh I am completing what is lacking in Christ’s afflictions for the sake of his body, that is, the church.
NRSVCE.25: I became its servant according to God’s commission that was given to me for you, to make the word of God fully known,
NRSVCE.26: the mystery that has been hidden throughout the ages and generations but has now been revealed to his saints.
NRSVCE.27: To them God chose to make known how great among the Gentiles are the riches of the glory of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
NRSVCE.28: It is he whom we proclaim, warning everyone and teaching everyone in all wisdom, so that we may present everyone mature in Christ.
NRSVCE.29: For this I toil and struggle with all the energy that he powerfully inspires within me.

NET.1: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
NET.2: to the saints, the faithful brothers and sisters in Christ, at Colossae. Grace and peace to you from God our Father!
NET.3: We always give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,
NET.4: since we heard about your faith in Christ Jesus and the love that you have for all the saints.
NET.5: Your faith and love have arisen from the hope laid up for you in heaven, which you have heard about in the message of truth, the gospel
NET.6: that has come to you. Just as in the entire world this gospel is bearing fruit and growing, so it has also been bearing fruit and growing among you from the first day you heard it and understood the grace of God in truth.
NET.7: You learned the gospel from Epaphras, our dear fellow slave — a faithful minister of Christ on our behalf —
NET.8: who also told us of your love in the Spirit.
NET.9: For this reason we also, from the day we heard about you, have not ceased praying for you and asking God to fill you with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
NET.10: so that you may live worthily of the Lord and please him in all respects — bearing fruit in every good deed, growing in the knowledge of God,
NET.11: being strengthened with all power according to his glorious might for the display of all patience and steadfastness, joyfully
NET.12: giving thanks to the Father who has qualified you to share in the saints’ inheritance in the light.
NET.13: He delivered us from the power of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of the Son he loves,
NET.14: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
NET.15: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn over all creation,
NET.16: for all things in heaven and on earth were created by him — all things, whether visible or invisible, whether thrones or dominions, whether principalities or powers — all things were created through him and for him.
NET.17: He himself is before all things and all things are held together in him.
NET.18: He is the head of the body, the church, as well as the beginning, the firstborn from among the dead, so that he himself may become first in all things.
NET.19: For God was pleased to have all his fullness dwell in the Son
NET.20: and through him to reconcile all things to himself by making peace through the blood of his cross — through him, whether things on earth or things in heaven.
NET.21: And you were at one time strangers and enemies in your minds as expressed through your evil deeds,
NET.22: but now he has reconciled you by his physical body through death to present you holy, without blemish, and blameless before him —
NET.23: if indeed you remain in the faith, established and firm, without shifting from the hope of the gospel that you heard. This gospel has also been preached in all creation under heaven, and I, Paul, have become its servant.
NET.24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for you, and I fill up in my physical body — for the sake of his body, the church — what is lacking in the sufferings of Christ.
NET.25: I became a servant of the church according to the stewardship from God — given to me for you — in order to complete the word of God,
NET.26: that is, the mystery that has been kept hidden from ages and generations, but has now been revealed to his saints.
NET.27: God wanted to make known to them the glorious riches of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
NET.28: We proclaim him by instructing and teaching all people with all wisdom so that we may present every person mature in Christ.
NET.29: Toward this goal I also labor, struggling according to his power that powerfully works in me.
